_comment: Real Supabase Database Webhook payloads, captured 2026-06-15 from Vercel logs after enabling the 8 webhooks on the paskateparks-dev project. revalidate-resolver.fixture.test.ts asserts each captured payload's shape so that if Supabase ever changes the envelope (or migration 0004 REPLICA IDENTITY FULL gets rolled back), we catch it loudly instead of silently dropping events. To capture more samples: trigger the table change in Studio, then look in Vercel Logs for the `[revalidate] payload: ...` line and paste the JSON after `payload:` into a new samples[] entry.

samples.label: UPDATE parks - status permanently_closed -> open on FDR (id=2)

record.name: FDR
record.hours:
April - October: 6:00am - 9:00pm, daily
November - March: 6:00am - 6:00pm, daily
record.description:
When it comes to DIY skate spots, FDR is the mecca. Whatever you're imagining in your head, this place is bigger; not just in square footage but the ramps are huge, fast and steep. Just outside the main park is smaller "slappy" street area by the parking lot with a bunch of goofy obstacles.

One of the things that makes FDR so difficult to skate is that there’s no pre-defined flow. It’s like the park is what it is and you just have to deal with it. Another thing that’s tough is how much dead space there is in the middle of the park. You really need to be cruising to get from one side to the other without pushing.
record.riding_surface_notes: There is often debris on the park when you show up. There are typically brooms available to clear the ramps.
